launcher.release_notes:
cx-labs-launcher: 0.10.1 — pin verify to active target + repair stale docker-credential-desktop (#107)

Two hotfixes on top of v0.10.0:

1. Check button probed the wrong cluster after target switch.

   The Go verify CLI (cx-cs-training-apps) runs `kubectl cluster-info`
   with no --context flag. That reads whichever context is `current`
   in ~/.kube/config — which is NOT synchronized with the launcher's
   target picker. Switching to Colima's cluster in the UI had no
   effect on kubectl's default, so Check kept probing whichever
   cluster kind's last `export kubeconfig` had set as current. With
   one cluster it worked by accident; with two it silently probed
   the wrong one.

   Fix: verify_scenario now sets KUBECONFIG on the Go CLI's env to
   the per-runtime kubeconfig file (kind-cx-labs-{colima,docker}.yaml)
   for managed-local runtime-suffixed contexts. Each of those files
   has its own current-context wired correctly, so the Go CLI's
   kubectl default now resolves to the cluster the user selected.
   External / legacy contexts fall through unchanged.

2. Docker-Desktop-uninstalled trap fixed in preflight.

   Docker Desktop's installer writes "credsStore":"desktop" into
   ~/.docker/config.json, telling Docker CLI to shell out to a
   helper binary that ships inside Docker.app. Uninstalling Docker
   Desktop rarely cleans that config, so every subsequent
   `docker pull` — including anonymous kindest/node pulls during
   `kind create cluster` — fails with:
     error getting credentials - err:
     exec: "docker-credential-desktop": executable file not found in $PATH
   Bites everyone who migrates Docker Desktop → Colima.

   New pre-flight step in install_local_cluster: if
   ~/.docker/config.json has credsStore=desktop AND
   docker-credential-desktop is missing from PATH, back the file
   up and remove the stale entry. Skipped in every other case
   (helper present, credsStore unset, JSON invalid, no config
   file). Never gates the install; the detail line lands in the
   step log.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
